rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RENNA HOLMES
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Tina Webster And Toshia Webster'S Motion To File An Amended Cross-Complaint Or, In The Alternative, Allowing Cross-Complaint Already Filed To Stand
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, DECEMBER 6, 2007, LINE 2, Defendants Tina Webster And Toshia Webster's Motion To File An Amended Cross-Complaint Or, In The Alternative, Allowing Cross-Complaint Already Filed To Stand IS GRANTED. THE CROSS-COMPLAINT IS COMPULSORY AND THE JUDICIAL POLICY IS LIBERAL IN ALLOWING THE AMENDMENT. NO PREJUDICE HAS BEEN SHOWN. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RENNA HOLMES
rulings.Calendar Matter: Cross Defendant Rhonda Colvin'S Motion To Strike Verified Cross-Complaint Of Tina Webster And Toshia Webster For Failure To Obtain Court Permission Before Filing, And As A Meritless Slapp And For Attorney'S Fees And Expenses
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, DECEMBER 6, 2007, LINE 2, Cross-Defendant Rhonda Colvin's Motion To Strike Verified Cross-Complaint Of Tina Webster And Toshia Webster For Failure To Obtain Court Permission Before Filing, And As A Meritless Slapp And For Attorney's Fees And Expenses IS DENIED. ALTHOUGH PARAGRAPH 11 ADDRESSES PROTECTED ACTIVITY, THE CROSS-COMPLAINT, TAKEN AS A WHOLE, FOCUSES ON MISAPPROPRIATION OF ASSETS OF HBCH THAT CONDUCT IS NOT PROTECTED ACTIVITY AND SO CROSS-DEFENDANT HAS FAILED TO MEET THE FIRST PRONG OF CCP SECTION 425.16. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: MARK MARTINI VS. TODD BELL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Ccp 872.720 Interlocutory Judgment & Appointment Of Referee In Partition Action
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, DECEMBER 6, 2007, LINE 7, PLAINTIFF MARK MARTINI'S Motion For CCP 872.720 Interlocutory Judgment & Appointment Of Referee In Partition Action IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO ESTABLISH AS A MATTER OF LAW THAT HE HAS AN OWNERSHIP INTEREST. THE FACTS ARE DISPUTED AS TO PLAINTIFF'S INTEREST AND THE DISPUTE CAN ONLY BE RESOLVED BY THE FILING OF A MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT BASED ON UNDISPUTED FACTS OR TRIAL. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: LELAND PORTEOUS DDS et al VS. LAWRENCE PORTEOUS DDS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Enforce Judgment;
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, DECEMBER 6, 2007, LINE 14, PETITIONERS LELAND PORTEOUS, LELAND F. PORTEOUS, D.D.S., P.C. ('DR. LEE') Motion To Enforce Judgment. The hearing is continued to December 21, 2007. The parties are to answer the following questions in writing on or before December 14, 2007: 1. How can the Court enforce paragraph 2(b) of the final award? 2. What is the consequence to each party of being required to "accept the obligations of the partnership or forfeit his right to the sale"? 3. Why should/should not paragraph 3 of the Final Award be enforced? 4. Dr. Lee's analysis of the economic terms concludes that Dr. Larry must pay Dr. Lee $169,569 and that Dr. Lee must pay Dr. Larry $88,607. Do you agree/disagree with the analysis and/or the calculations? If so, explain. 5. Can the Court order payments of the amounts set forth in question 4? Why/why not? 6. What authority does the Court have to order an accounting? 7. What basis does Dr. Lee have for objecting to including the arbitrator's decision regarding the treatment of patients in connection with the sale of the Vallejo practice? 8. Why are/are not the amounts owed by Dr. Lee subject to being set off against what Dr. Larry owes to Dr. Lee? 9. What provisions have either side insisted on that are at odds with the arbitration award? Identify the term(s) and the applicable arbitration award provision(s)? 10. Each party is to submit a proposed form of order that reflects their position as to how this motion should be resolved. 11. Should the parties be referred to the arbitrator for the crafting of an order enforcing the judgment? 12. Should the parties be required to mediate their differences before Judge Dossee, a Judge of this Court or another mediator to be selected by the Court? =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: ROBIN WEARY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, DECEMBER 6, 2007, LINE 15, PETITIONER ROBIN WEARY'S Motion For Order Compelling Arbitration IS GRANTED. IT WAS THE INTENT OF THE PARTIES TO ARBITRATE THIS AND RELATED DISPUTES. THE FACT THAT THE PROPERTY HAS BEEN SOLD DOES NOT CHANGE THAT FACT OR THE ENFORCEABILITY OF THE AGREEMENT TO ARBITRATE. DEFENDANT HAS FAILED TO DEMONSTRATE WAIVER. =(302/PJM/PB)
